target: data/wins.json [034].detail_evidence — fix skin-depth frequency: '0.066 mm at 1 GHz' → '0.002 mm (2 μm) at 1 GHz'

patches.find_pattern: copper's skin depth is ~0.066 mm at 1 GHz
patches.replace_pattern: copper's skin depth is ~0.002 mm (2 μm) at 1 GHz
patches.description: Correct 3-order-of-magnitude frequency error: 0.066 mm is the copper skin depth at 1 MHz, not 1 GHz. At 1 GHz the formula δ = sqrt(ρ/πfμ₀) gives δ ≈ 2.06 μm = 0.002 mm. This is consistent with WIN-048's value (~0.2 μm at 160 GHz, which scales as 2 μm × sqrt(1GHz/160GHz) ≈ 0.16 μm). The qualitative conclusion (copper opaque to all radio) remains correct — a 2 μm skin depth still means even a thin copper shell is many skin depths thick.

tldr_evidence: Copper skin depth at 1 GHz is ~2 μm (0.002 mm), not 0.066 mm. The latter is the 1 MHz value; frequency differs by 3 orders of magnitude. Fixed. Qualitative conclusion unaffected.
tldr_verdict: No verdict change — WIN-034 finding (dome's copper canopy claim is self-refuting) stands unchanged. Patch corrects a supporting metric value only.
